questions.question: Why does a 2D+pool video model fail on the Something-Something V2 dataset?
questions.question_zh: 为什么「2D + 池化」式的视频模型在 Something-Something V2 数据集上会失败？
options: The images are too small
The model architecture is incompatible

The dataset's labels are defined by motion direction ('pushing something from left to right'), not appearance; order-invariant average pooling over per-frame features cannot distinguish motion direction
There are too many classes
options_zh: 图片太小了
模型架构不兼容
该数据集的标签是按运动方向定义的（如「把某物从左推到右」），不是外观；对每帧特征做的顺序无关平均池化没法区分运动方向
类别太多
questions.explanation: Something-Something labels are literally 'X moved left to right' vs 'X moved right to left'. The two look identical in any single frame, and averaging frame embeddings is order-invariant so pool(f1, f2, ..., fT) == pool(fT, ..., f2, f1). Motion-defined labels require a model that attends to temporal order.
questions.explanation_zh: Something-Something 的标签字面上就是「X 从左到右移动」与「X 从右到左移动」。任意单帧两者看起来完全一样，再加上对帧 embedding 取平均与顺序无关，于是 pool(f1, f2, ..., fT) == pool(fT, ..., f2, f1)。要区分这种运动定义的标签，模型必须关注时间顺序。

questions.question: What is I3D's inflation trick?
options: A way to pretrain on more images

Take a 2D CNN's pretrained kernels, copy each along a new time axis (dividing by kernel_T to preserve activation scale), and use them to initialise a 3D CNN — giving the 3D model strong weights without 3D pretraining
A scheduler for learning rates
A method for compressing videos
options_zh: 一种用更多图像做预训练的方法
把 2D CNN 预训练好的 kernel 沿新引入的时间轴复制（同时除以 kernel_T 保持激活尺度），用来初始化 3D CNN，让 3D 模型不靠 3D 预训练就拿到强权重
一种学习率调度器
一种视频压缩方法
questions.explanation: Inflation bootstraps 3D video models from strong 2D ImageNet weights. A 3x3 2D kernel becomes a 3x3x3 3D kernel by replicating along T with a 1/kernel_T rescale. It transfers object and texture features learnt on ImageNet directly into the video model, which is why I3D was the first 3D model to seriously beat 2D+pool baselines.
questions.explanation_zh: inflation 让 3D 视频模型直接继承强 2D ImageNet 权重。一个 3x3 的 2D kernel 沿时间轴复制并按 1/kernel_T 缩放，就变成 3x3x3 的 3D kernel。这样 ImageNet 学到的物体与纹理特征直接迁移到视频模型，I3D 也成为第一个真正大幅超越「2D + 池化」基线的 3D 模型。

questions.question: A (2+1)D factorised convolution splits a 3D conv into which two operations?
questions.question_zh: (2+1)D 分解卷积把一个 3D 卷积拆成哪两个操作？
options: One temporal conv followed by one depthwise conv
A grouped conv and a 1x1 conv
Two 3D convs with different strides
A spatial 1x3x3 conv followed by a temporal 3x1x1 conv, with a BN+ReLU in between to add a non-linearity that full 3D convs do not have
options_zh: 一个时间卷积接一个 depthwise 卷积
一个 grouped conv 加一个 1x1 conv
两个不同 stride 的 3D 卷积
先做一个空间上的 1x3x3 卷积，再做一个时间上的 3x1x1 卷积，中间夹一个 BN+ReLU，引入完整 3D 卷积所没有的额外非线性
questions.explanation: (2+1)D factorises the 3x3x3 kernel into (1x3x3) then (3x1x1). The two convs have a non-linearity between them (BN+ReLU), which increases expressive power per parameter. On Kinetics, R(2+1)D-34 outperforms an equivalent R3D-34 with fewer params — the extra non-linearity is doing real work.
questions.explanation_zh: (2+1)D 把 3x3x3 的 kernel 拆成 (1x3x3) 再 (3x1x1)。两个卷积之间夹了一层非线性（BN+ReLU），让每个参数的表达能力更强。在 Kinetics 上，R(2+1)D-34 用更少参数就能超越同规模的 R3D-34，多出来的那层非线性确实在干活。

questions.question: In a video transformer, what does 'divided attention' mean?
questions.question_zh: 在视频 transformer 中，「divided attention」是什么意思？
options: Attention over half the tokens

Each transformer block has two attention modules: one over tokens at the same spatial position across time (temporal attention), then one over tokens at the same timestep across space (spatial attention) — breaking the O((T*H*W)^2) full attention into O(T^2) + O((H*W)^2)
Attention applied only during training
Attention that skips some layers
options_zh: 对一半 token 做 attention
每个 transformer block 里有两个 attention 模块：先在时间维上对同一空间位置的 token 做时间 attention，再在同一时间步内对所有空间位置的 token 做空间 attention，把 O((T*H*W)^2) 的全 attention 拆成 O(T^2) + O((H*W)^2)
只在训练时使用 attention
跳过某些层的 attention
questions.explanation: Full joint spatio-temporal attention is O((T*H*W)^2), which is infeasible for long videos. Divided attention (TimeSformer) alternates temporal and spatial attention within each block, bringing cost to O(T^2 + (H*W)^2). It trades off a theoretical loss in expressivity for tractable training; in practice divided attention matches or beats joint on most benchmarks.
questions.explanation_zh: 完整的时空联合 attention 复杂度是 O((T*H*W)^2)，长视频根本算不动。divided attention（TimeSformer）在每个 block 内交替做时间和空间 attention，把代价降到 O(T^2 + (H*W)^2)。理论上牺牲了一点表达力，但训练变得可行；实践中 divided attention 在大多数基准上能追平甚至超过 joint。

questions.question: Your Kinetics-400 model reports 76% clip accuracy and 82% video accuracy. What does the gap tell you?
questions.question_zh: 你的 Kinetics-400 模型 clip 准确率 76%、video 准确率 82%，这个差距说明了什么？
options: Per-clip predictions are noisy; averaging predictions across multiple sampled clips per video (test-time augmentation) stabilises the result. A large gap suggests the model's features are sensitive to which 8-frame window was sampled, and longer clips or stronger spatial augmentation during training would shrink the gap
The model is broken
The test set is too small
Clip accuracy is always lower than video accuracy by definition
options_zh: 单个 clip 的预测有噪声；对每段视频多个采样 clip 的预测做平均（测试时增广）会更稳定。差距大说明模型特征对采样到哪 8 帧窗口很敏感，训练时用更长的 clip 或更强的空间增广能把差距缩小
模型坏了
测试集太小
按定义 clip 准确率永远低于 video 准确率
questions.explanation: Clip accuracy evaluates the model on a single sampled window; video accuracy averages predictions across multiple windows. A 6-point gap means the model is sensitive to which window you sampled. Closing the gap means the model has generalised better across the temporal distribution of each video — which is what you want in deployment. Report both numbers always.
questions.explanation_zh: clip 准确率是在单个采样窗口上的评估；video 准确率是在每段视频多个窗口预测的平均。6 个点的差距说明模型对采样到哪段窗口很敏感。缩小这个差距等于模型在每段视频的时序分布上泛化得更好——这正是部署时想要的。两个数字应该一起报告。
